summary: Iter4/iter28 — verified the prod root-cause fix: still avatar image (JPG/PNG) is now converted to a 1-frame GIF (supported sync-lipsync video format) via Pillow, paired with sync_mode='loop' (env-overridable via FAL_LIPSYNC_SYNC_MODE), and the admin UI shows the FULL 2000-char fal HTTP body inside an expandable <details> panel (replacing the CSS-truncated chip). All 17 unit tests in test_avatar_lipsync_image_upload.py pass (including 4 new GIF tests: JPG→GIF, PNG-RGBA→GIF, 4000x4000 downscale to ≤1024, fallback on garbage). Backend /api/avatar-chat/status now returns lipsync_sync_mode='loop'. End-to-end /send→/job as admin returns video_status=completed, audio_url populated, error_code=PROVIDER_AUTH_FAILED, lipsync_debug='no_fal_key' (expected — no FAL_KEY in preview). Frontend /video-avatar-chat as admin shows FALLBACK (TEXT) pill, PROVIDER_AUTH_FAILED chip, job:avj_... chip, and the new <details data-testid=avatar-lipsync-debug-panel-<id>> with summary 'show provider response' — expanding it renders the full body in <pre data-testid=avatar-lipsync-debug-<id>> + a copy button. Non-admin (sr-tester) is correctly subscription-gated by the existing paywall, so the admin-only debug panel cannot render — visibility check `isAdmin && msg.lipsync_debug` at VideoAvatarChat.jsx:75 is the correct gate. Pillow 12.2.0 confirmed available. No JS console errors during admin send flow.
critical_code_review_comments: avatar_chat.py L202 — _image_bytes_to_gif() uses Pillow with proper RGBA→RGB flatten, thumbnail downscale to 1024, and a try/except that falls back to original bytes (content_type=image/jpeg) on bad input. Solid defensive coding.

avatar_chat.py L345 — GIF conversion is called BEFORE fal_client.upload_file at L382; image_bytes_for_upload/image_ct_for_upload override the original bytes so the uploaded asset is always a GIF on the sync-lipsync path. RCA fix is in the correct place.

avatar_chat.py L398-405 — submit_args contains only {video_url, audio_url}; sync_mode added only when FAL_LIPSYNC_SYNC_MODE is non-empty (default 'loop'); model only when FAL_LIPSYNC_MODEL is set. This is the right shape to avoid Pydantic 422 from the v1 endpoint.

VideoAvatarChat.jsx L75-94 — admin-gated <details> with summary 'show provider response ▾', scrollable <pre> with whitespace-pre-wrap (renders the full multi-line body), copy button with navigator.clipboard?.writeText optional chaining. data-testids correctly include the message_id suffix. The element is rendered only when `isAdmin && msg.lipsync_debug` is truthy, so non-admins never see it.

avatar_chat.py is now 1098 lines (was 1043 last iter); still above the 700-line guidance — consider splitting the fal client glue (classifier + lipsync helpers + image helpers) into a fal_lipsync.py submodule in a follow-up refactor.

Non-blocking: GIF conversion uses Image.thumbnail (mutates in place) before saving with optimize=True — good. The function also passes the original bytes through if Pillow can't decode (e.g., binary garbage), which is what test_image_bytes_to_gif_fallback_on_bad_input asserts. No issues.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (admin debug panel renders + expands + copy button works; non-admin correctly gated)

test_credentials: Admin: krajapraveen@gmail.com / TestPass123! ; Non-admin: sr-tester@example.com / TestPass123! (subscription-gated — paywall modal shown, which is expected behavior).
seed_data_creation: None — used existing demo clone slug 'raja-demo' for end-to-end backend send→job test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter4/iter28 fix is verified end-to-end on preview. The GIF conversion code path is exercised by 4 dedicated unit tests; the live fal submit cannot run on preview (no FAL_KEY) so the helper short-circuits with no_fal_key. When prod gets FAL_KEY, retest by sending an avatar message: lipsync_debug on failure should now contain 'submit:HTTP 422 body=[{...}]' with the FULL Pydantic detail list (no longer CSS-truncated in the admin chip — expand the <details> panel to read it). If 422 still occurs with GIF + sync_mode='loop', the next likely culprit is the GIF being too small/short (try sync_mode='cut_off' or feeding a 2-frame GIF). Pre-existing 3 failures in tests/test_avatar_chat.py (subscription gate) remain unrelated and unchanged.
rca of the issue: Root cause of the production FALLBACK (TEXT) PROVIDER_422 with CSS-truncated body: (a) the avatar still image (JPG from i.pravatar.cc or user upload) was being uploaded directly to fal-ai/sync-lipsync as `video_url`, but the endpoint's Pydantic schema validates `video_url` MIME against a video allowlist (mp4/mov/webm/m4v/gif) — JPG/PNG triggers 422; (b) the admin chip used CSS text-truncate, so the full Pydantic body was visible only on hover. Fix (iter4/iter28): convert image bytes → 1-frame GIF via Pillow before upload (since GIF is in the video allowlist), pair with sync_mode='loop' so fal repeats the frame to match audio duration, and replace the truncated chip with a full <details> panel + copy button. Reproduction with FAL_KEY set: prior to this fix, POST /api/avatar-chat/send with any JPG avatar would produce lipsync_debug='submit:HTTP 422 body=[{"loc":["body","video_url"],...}]'; after the fix, the JPG is converted to GIF in _image_bytes_to_gif() and the schema check passes, so the submit succeeds and the lipsync job runs to completion.
